recommendation: Reduce over-concentration in Other Asia, nes / Taiwan-proxy semiconductor imports by identifying alternative suppliers for HS8542 inputs where feasible.
target_industries: Electronics, Automotive, HDD / Storage, Home Appliances
rationale: Phase 2 shows Other Asia, nes share increased from 22.8% in 2014 to 43.6% in 2024, creating a high concentration risk.
implementation_actions: Map top IC inputs by product line; qualify secondary suppliers in Korea, Japan, Malaysia, Singapore, and China where technically feasible; create dual-sourcing rules for critical components.
expected_benefit: Lower exposure to a single geography and improve supply resilience.

recommendation: Create inventory buffers for critical semiconductor components with long lead times and limited substitutes.
rationale: Phase 3 indicates automotive and HDD have bottleneck risk: even low-cost chips can stop production of high-value finished goods.
implementation_actions: Classify chips into critical/non-critical; set safety stock policies for critical ICs; review inventory levels quarterly based on lead-time signals.
expected_benefit: Reduce short-term production disruption during allocation periods.

recommendation: Develop a semiconductor supply chain risk dashboard tracking import dependency, supplier concentration, and industry revenue exposure.
rationale: The project demonstrates that import dependency and industry exposure can be monitored using public data and reproducible analytics.
implementation_actions: Update UN Comtrade/WITS data annually; monitor HS8542 supplier shares; combine with industry production indicators from OIE/BOI.
expected_benefit: Improve early-warning visibility for firms and policymakers.

recommendation: Support semiconductor-related capabilities in assembly, testing, PCB, power electronics, and advanced electronics manufacturing.
rationale: Thailand may not replace advanced Taiwan foundry capacity, but can reduce downstream vulnerability by strengthening regional semiconductor-related ecosystems.
implementation_actions: Encourage investment incentives for OSAT, PCB, electronics components, and power devices; promote workforce development in semiconductor-related manufacturing.
expected_benefit: Strengthen Thailand’s position in the semiconductor-adjacent supply chain.

recommendation: Institutionalize semiconductor disruption scenario planning using mild/moderate/severe stress tests.
target_industries: Electronics, Automotive, HDD / Storage, Home Appliances
rationale: Phase 4 shows total modeled impact could range from USD 0.83B under mild stress to USD 3.34B under severe stress across the four selected industries.
implementation_actions: Run annual tabletop exercises; test 10%, 20%, and 40% disruption assumptions; link results to procurement and production planning.
expected_benefit: Convert risk analysis into actionable contingency planning.
